choice: Probability of not running out of money through life expectancy via Monte Carlo
rationale: Two binding priors converge on this answer. Decision 2aa23484 establishes the app replaces an existing Excel model — the decision context confirms that model runs 10 000 Monte Carlo trials and reports P(assets>0) at checkpoint ages, so continuity with the incumbent tool points directly to this metric. Decision 479caa25 establishes the headline job-to-be-done as knowing 'how they are tracking' against the retirement plan, and the user's own framing of the primary question ('will I run out of money in retirement?') maps precisely to a survival probability, not a net-worth level or a withdrawal-rate scalar. A single deterministic projection cannot capture the uncertainty the user cares about, and a safe-withdrawal-rate number is a planning input rather than a tracking output — neither serves the 'actual vs plan' core job as directly as a Monte Carlo survival probability.
